Patch Set 6:

Is there a reason mkfs_lustre and tunefs_lustre share a main()?  Wouldn't it make more sense to have a mkfs_lustre.c, a tunefs_lustre.c and a common file to handle the functions that are shared between the two (some of the argument parsing, help messages, etc.)

As it is now, it violates the "it should do what it says on the tin" portion of the style guidelines.

Patch Set 6:

Ben, I'm not wholly against that kind of change. They are combined today because most of the options and functionality are the same between the two tools.  I admit it has caused me some worries in the past if somehow "mkfs" is accidentally run on a filesystem when "tunefs" was intended, so having them be separate tools, where mkfs never appears in the tunefs code, would be good. 

That said, that should be a separate patch from this one, which I think is a good cleanup and would also simplify the code separation in a later patch, so I think it is worthwhile to land as-is.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Looks good to me

Patch Set 7:

(5 comments)

Some minor improvements are possible if the patch is refreshed, but anything more complex than fixing the whitespace and reordering the options to be in alphabetical order and segregated by mkfs/tunefs should really be in a separate patch.

I'd really rather land this patch as-is, since it makes the code much better already, and then take a second pass at further cleanups once this huge reordering is out of the way.

commit.subject: LU-5070 utils: mkfs.lustre code cleanup
commit.message:
LU-5070 utils: mkfs.lustre code cleanup

Re-order the options to increase the code readability.
Remove unused code, redundant logic, white spaces,
blank lines, and so on.
